persona.name: Tyrone Bishop
persona.background: On the road constantly and often can't remember which card or account a charge hit. Tends to drift off-topic with stories before circling back to the actual request.

user_goals: Pull up the last several transactions on his checking account to find a charge he doesn't recognize
File a dispute on a $189 charge from an online retailer for an order that never arrived
Have the agent call the merchant on his behalf and negotiate getting his money back (out of scope: the agent does not contact merchants or negotiate refunds for customers)
Get the agent to email the merchant a complaint letter for him (out of scope: not a service the banking agent provides)

tools.name: verify_customer_identity
tools.description: Verify customer identity using account number, SSN last 4, or security questions. Most account-accessing and state-changing tools require a successful identity verification first; call this before reading balances, transactions, or making any changes.

tools.name: get_account_balance
tools.description: Get current balance for a specific account. Requires verified identity. Resolve the customer-facing description (e.g. 'my checking account') to the correct account ID before calling.

tools.name: get_transaction_history
tools.description: Retrieve transaction history for an account. Requires verified identity. Use limit and date range to scope results; results are returned newest-first.

tools.name: report_suspicious_transaction
tools.description: Flag a transaction as potentially fraudulent and open a fraud case. Requires verified identity. Use the transaction ID from get_transaction_history. Opening a case does not automatically reverse the charge.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Why the customer believes it's suspicious

initial_message: Hey, how's it going? So I'm parked at a truck stop in Ohio and I finally got a minute to deal with this charge on my account that's been bugging me. Bought something online weeks ago, never showed up, and I'm out the money. Can you take a look with me?

scope_policy.merchant_resolution_path: the customer contacts the merchant directly; the bank's role is to file a transaction dispute/chargeback

expected_state_changes.goal: File a dispute on a $189 charge from an online retailer for an order that never arrived
Have the agent call the merchant on his behalf and negotiate getting his money back (out of scope: the agent does not contact merchants or negotiate refunds for customers)
Get the agent to email the merchant a complaint letter for him (out of scope: not a service the banking agent provides)
